metadata.note: Archetype B (bilateral): the engagement/hostility track runs in both directions -- Seoul's overtures, Pyongyang's responses, incidents on the line -- so no valid primary_target. Alias purity is therefore the only lever: fixed toponyms, named agreements and own-side institutions only. Bare 'unification' EXCLUDED -- it is a homograph trap that pulls the Japanese Unification Church dissolution case (roughly a third of all 'unification' hits). 'reunification' is safe (the Church is never called that) and 'Unification Minist' is the shortest form covering Ministry/Minister. Bare 'drone' EXCLUDED: no target gate here, so it would pull the whole Ukraine drone corpus -- the DPRK drone-incursion story carries 'Unification Minist' anyway.
metadata.audit_2026_07_16: fr 'tract' DROPPED -- rule 6b substring collision: 'tract' is a substring of 'contract' and matched Hanwha submarine-contract stories (n=12, 42% foreign). Kept despite high nominal %foreign, verified benign co-mention: 'Unification Minist' (39%, samples all the ROK ministry), ja 南北対話 (100%, the co-tag is the Vatican -- president/Pope discussing inter-Korean dialogue), it 'riunificazione' (100%, the co-tag is a Trump mention in an on-topic NK-constitution story).

bundle.term: Named agreements and the engagement track (Pillar 3)
